entries.name: SEA LIFE Sydney Aquarium
entries.desc: A major public aquarium on Sydney's waterfront, known for its underwater tunnels and dugongs, showcasing marine life drawn largely from Australian waters including the Great Barrier Reef.

photo.alt: Darling Harbour waterfront at night, Sydney
photo.credit: <!-- photo: darling-harbour.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Sydney (AU), Darling Harbour -- 2019 -- 3202-4.jpg" by Dietmar Rabich · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sydney_(AU),_Darling_Harbour_--_2019_--_3202-4.jpg -->

entries.name: AquaRio
entries.desc: Opened as part of Rio's waterfront regeneration for the 2016 Olympics, it is the largest aquarium in South America, centered on a 12-meter acrylic tunnel with sharks, rays, and open-ocean species.

photo.alt: Museum of Tomorrow in Rio de Janeiro's Porto Maravilha waterfront district
photo.credit: <!-- photo: museu-do-amanha.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Museu do Amanhã 05.JPG" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Museu_do_Amanh%C3%A3_05.JPG -->

entries.name: Ripley's Aquarium of Canada
entries.desc: A large downtown aquarium next to the CN Tower whose signature attraction is a long moving walkway that carries visitors through a tunnel surrounded by sharks and other predators.

photo.alt: The CN Tower in downtown Toronto
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cn-tower.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "CN Tower, Toronto, Ontario (29969151776).jpg" by Ken Lund · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:CN_Tower,_Toronto,_Ontario_(29969151776).jpg -->

entries.name: Vancouver Aquarium
entries.desc: Located in Stanley Park, this is one of Canada's oldest and largest aquariums, long recognized as a center for marine research, conservation, and public education about ocean life.

photo.alt: Totem poles in Stanley Park, Vancouver
photo.credit: <!-- photo: stanley-park.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Stanley Park, Vancouver (7889962542).jpg" by InSapphoWeTrust · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Stanley_Park,_Vancouver_(7889962542).jpg -->

entries.name: Shanghai Ocean Aquarium
entries.desc: Set in the Pudong district near the Oriental Pearl Tower, it is organized around regional zones and features one of the world's longest underwater viewing tunnels.

photo.alt: Oriental Pearl Tower in Shanghai's Pudong district
photo.credit: <!-- photo: oriental-pearl-tower.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Shanghai Oriental Pearl Tower.JPG" by MiLu24 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Shanghai_Oriental_Pearl_Tower.JPG -->

meta: One of World's Longest Underwater Tunnels

entries.name: Den Blå Planet
entries.desc: Denmark's national aquarium, whose distinctive whirlpool-shaped building near Copenhagen's airport houses freshwater and marine exhibits ranging from Amazon rivers to open-ocean tanks.

photo.alt: Den Blå Planet aquarium building in Kastrup, Denmark
photo.credit: <!-- photo: den-bla-planet.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Den Blå Planet (Kastrup).JPG" by Orf3us · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Den_Bl%C3%A5_Planet_(Kastrup).JPG -->

entries.name: Nausicaá
entries.desc: A national sea center on France's Channel coast dedicated to raising awareness of the oceans, built around vast tanks that recreate open-water and coral reef environments.

photo.alt: Exterior of the Nausicaá sea center in Boulogne-sur-Mer, France
photo.credit: <!-- photo: nausicaa-boulogne-sur-mer.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "00 3102 Marine aquarium \"Nausicaä\" in Boulogne-sur-Mer, France.jpg" by W.Bulach · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:00_3102_Marine_aquarium_%22Nausica%C3%A4%22_in_Boulogne-sur-Mer,_France.jpg -->

entries.name: Ozeaneum
entries.desc: A critically acclaimed natural history of the sea museum on the Baltic coast, winner of the 2010 European Museum of the Year Award, known for its dramatic architecture and large open-ocean tanks focused on North Atlantic and Baltic marine life.

photo.alt: Ozeaneum aquarium building entrance in Stralsund
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ozeaneum-stralsund.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Ozeaneum in Stralsund, Eingang.JPG" by Klugschnacker · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ozeaneum_in_Stralsund,_Eingang.JPG -->

entries.name: Acquario di Genova
entries.desc: A centerpiece of Genoa's redeveloped Old Port, designed by Renzo Piano, it is one of Europe's largest aquariums and a leading draw for visitors to the Italian Riviera city.

photo.alt: Acquario di Genova building at the Old Port
photo.credit: <!-- photo: acquario-di-genova.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Acquario di Genova - panoramio.jpg" by kajikawa · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Acquario_di_Genova_-_panoramio.jpg -->

entries.name: Okinawa Churaumi Aquarium
entries.desc: Set within Ocean Expo Park in northern Okinawa, it is renowned for its huge Kuroshio tank, one of the largest in the world, home to whale sharks and manta rays.

photo.alt: Okinawa Churaumi Aquarium building viewed from the west
photo.credit: <!-- photo: okinawa-churaumi-aquarium.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Okinawa Churaumi Aquarium from west.JPG" by そらみみ · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Okinawa_Churaumi_Aquarium_from_west.JPG -->

entries.name: Osaka Aquarium Kaiyukan
entries.desc: One of Japan's largest aquariums, arranged so visitors spiral downward around a central Pacific Ocean tank, recreating the marine habitats of the Ring of Fire.

photo.alt: Osaka Aquarium Kaiyukan and Tempozan Market Place building at the harbour
photo.credit: <!-- photo: osaka-aquarium-kaiyukan.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Tempozan Market Place Building and Osaka Aquarium "Kaiyukan".jpg" by そらみみ · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tempozan_Market_Place_Building_and_Osaka_Aquarium_%22Kaiyukan%22.jpg -->

entries.name: Aquaria KLCC
entries.desc: Set beneath the Kuala Lumpur City Centre park in the shadow of the Petronas Twin Towers, it features one of Southeast Asia's longest underwater tunnels and a wide variety of freshwater and marine habitats.

photo.alt: Petronas Twin Towers above Kuala Lumpur City Centre
photo.credit: <!-- photo: petronas-twin-towers-klcc.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Petronas Towers view2, Kuala Lumpur.jpg" · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Petronas_Towers_view2,_Kuala_Lumpur.jpg -->

meta: One of Southeast Asia's Longest Underwater Tunnels

entries.name: Kelly Tarlton's Sea Life Aquarium
entries.desc: Built inside decommissioned sewage holding tanks on Auckland's waterfront, it is best known for a walk-through Antarctic exhibit with king penguins and a curved shark-and-ray tunnel.

photo.alt: Entrance to Kelly Tarlton's Sea Life Aquarium on the Auckland waterfront
photo.credit: <!-- photo: kelly-tarltons-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Entrance to Kelly Tarlton's Sea Life Aquarium.jpg" by Jonathan Harker · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Entrance_to_Kelly_Tarlton%27s_Sea_Life_Aquarium.jpg -->

entries.name: Atlanterhavsparken
entries.desc: Norway's largest aquarium sits on a rocky peninsula outside Ålesund with tanks fed directly by Atlantic seawater, and outdoor areas for penguins and seals alongside deep-sea Atlantic exhibits.

photo.alt: Atlanterhavsparken aquarium building on the Tueneset peninsula near Ålesund
photo.credit: <!-- photo: atlanterhavsparken-alesund.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Atlantikpark Gebäude Alesund.jpg" by Olaf Meister · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Atlantikpark_Geb%C3%A4ude_Alesund.jpg -->

entries.name: Oceanário de Lisboa
entries.desc: Built for the 1998 World Expo in Lisbon's riverside Parque das Nações, it centers on a single enormous ocean tank surrounded by habitats representing the world's oceans.

photo.alt: Oceanário de Lisboa building in Parque das Nações
photo.credit: <!-- photo: oceanario-de-lisboa.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.5 · "Oceanário de Lisboa.JPG" by Paulo Juntas · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ocean%C3%A1rio_de_Lisboa.JPG -->

entries.name: S.E.A. Aquarium
entries.desc: Part of the Resorts World Sentosa complex, this large aquarium is built around a vast open-ocean habitat viewed through one of the world's biggest acrylic windows.

photo.alt: Resorts World Sentosa complex on Sentosa Island, Singapore
photo.credit: <!-- photo: resorts-world-sentosa.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Resorts World Sentosa, Singapore - 20101215.jpg" by WiNG · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Resorts_World_Sentosa,_Singapore_-_20101215.jpg -->

entries.name: Two Oceans Aquarium
entries.desc: On Cape Town's V&A Waterfront, it showcases the marine life where the Atlantic and Indian Oceans meet, with a large predator exhibit and a strong conservation focus.

photo.alt: Victorian Clock Tower at the V&A Waterfront, Cape Town
photo.credit: <!-- photo: va-waterfront-clock-tower.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Victorian Clock Tower, Table Bay Harbour, Cape Town V&A Waterfront, Western Cape. 1883. Gothic Style. 08.JPG" by A3alb · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Victorian_Clock_Tower,_Table_Bay_Harbour,_Cape_Town_V%26A_Waterfront,_Western_Cape._1883._Gothic_Style._08.JPG -->

entries.name: COEX Aquarium
entries.desc: Set inside the vast COEX Mall in Gangnam, this is one of Seoul's most visited attractions, with 650+ species arranged in themed zones spread across the underground shopping complex.

photo.alt: Courtyard entrance to COEX Mall in Gangnam, Seoul
photo.credit: <!-- photo: coex-mall-entrance.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "COEX Mall entry (5464343844).jpg" by InSapphoWeTrust · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:COEX_Mall_entry_(5464343844).jpg -->

entries.name: L'Oceanogràfic
entries.tag: City of Arts and Sciences, Valencia
entries.desc: Part of Valencia's City of Arts and Sciences, this oceanographic park is organized into buildings representing the planet's main marine ecosystems, from Arctic to tropical seas.

photo.alt: L'Oceanogràfic building viewed across the water, City of Arts and Sciences, Valencia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: oceanografic-valencia.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "L'Oceanografic, Valencia, Spain 1 - Jan 07.jpg" by Diliff · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:L%27Oceanografic,_Valencia,_Spain_1_-_Jan_07.jpg -->

entries.name: L'Aquàrium de Barcelona
entries.desc: Located in the Port Vell marina, it focuses on Mediterranean marine life and is best known for a long glass tunnel that lets visitors walk beneath sharks and rays.

photo.alt: Port Vell marina in Barcelona
photo.credit: <!-- photo: port-vell-barcelona.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.5 · "Barcelona Port Vell 03.JPG" by böhringer friedrich · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Barcelona_Port_Vell_03.JPG -->

entries.name: SEA LIFE Bangkok Ocean World
entries.desc: One of Southeast Asia's largest aquariums, occupying the basement levels of the Siam Paragon mall, with diverse marine zones and experiences including shark dives and a glass-bottom boat ride.

photo.alt: Siam Paragon mall in Bangkok
photo.credit: <!-- photo: siam-paragon.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.5 · "Siam Paragon.JPG" by Terence Ong · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Siam_Paragon.JPG -->

entries.name: SEA LIFE London Aquarium
entries.desc: Occupying the former Greater London Council chambers in County Hall directly facing the Houses of Parliament, it is one of Europe's largest collections of global sea life and a flagship South Bank attraction.

photo.alt: County Hall, South Bank, London, seen from Westminster Bridge
photo.credit: <!-- photo: county-hall-london.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "County Hall, London (West Face - 01).jpg" by Doyle of London · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:County_Hall,_London_(West_Face_-_01).jpg -->

entries.name: Georgia Aquarium
entries.desc: One of the largest aquariums in the world, it is among the few outside Asia to keep whale sharks, housed in a massive tank alongside manta rays and other open-ocean species.

photo.alt: Georgia Aquarium exterior entrance, Atlanta
photo.credit: <!-- photo: georgia-aquarium.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.5 · "Georgia Aquarium Jan 2006.jpg" by Diliff · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Georgia_Aquarium_Jan_2006.jpg -->

entries.name: Monterey Bay Aquarium
entries.desc: Built on a former cannery site on Monterey Bay, it is widely praised for its towering kelp forest tank and its pioneering work in ocean conservation and animal rescue.

photo.alt: Monterey Bay Aquarium entrance on Cannery Row, Monterey
photo.credit: <!-- photo: monterey-bay-aquarium.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Monterey Bay Aquarium Front.JPG" by Ardo191 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Monterey_Bay_Aquarium_Front.JPG -->

entries.name: Shedd Aquarium
entries.desc: A landmark on Chicago's lakefront Museum Campus, this historic aquarium houses a wide range of freshwater and marine species, including a large oceanarium for marine mammals.

photo.alt: John G. Shedd Aquarium facade, Chicago
photo.credit: <!-- photo: shedd-aquarium.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Shedd Aquarium 2018.jpg" by Chris6d · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Shedd_Aquarium_2018.jpg -->

entries.name: National Aquarium
entries.desc: A centerpiece of Baltimore's Inner Harbor, it is known for its multi-story rooftop rainforest and a spiraling shark exhibit, drawing visitors as a major regional attraction.

photo.alt: National Aquarium at Baltimore's Inner Harbor
photo.credit: <!-- photo: national-aquarium-baltimore.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "BaltimoreNationalAquarium.JPG" by AndrewHorne · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:BaltimoreNationalAquarium.JPG -->

entries.name: New England Aquarium
entries.desc: A waterfront institution on Boston Harbor built around a four-story cylindrical coral reef tank, one of the oldest and most recognized aquariums in the United States and a model for urban marine education.

photo.alt: New England Aquarium on Boston Harbor
photo.credit: <!-- photo: new-england-aquarium.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "2017 New England Aquarium from Boston Harbor.jpg" by Beyond My Ken · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:2017_New_England_Aquarium_from_Boston_Harbor.jpg -->

entries.name: Tennessee Aquarium
entries.desc: On the banks of the Tennessee River, it is home to the world's largest freshwater aquarium building alongside a separate ocean journey wing, covering habitats from Appalachian rivers to tropical seas.

photo.alt: Tennessee Aquarium's River Journey building, Chattanooga
photo.credit: <!-- photo: tennessee-aquarium.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "TN Aquarium.jpg" by Ɱ · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:TN_Aquarium.jpg -->

entries.name: Birch Aquarium at Scripps
entries.desc: Perched on a bluff above the Pacific at UC San Diego's Scripps Institution of Oceanography, it blends research and public education with exhibits focused on Pacific Ocean and California coastal habitats.

photo.alt: Birch Aquarium at Scripps entrance plaza, La Jolla
photo.credit: <!-- photo: birch-aquarium-scripps.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Birch Aquarium Entrance.JPG" by Takinzinnia · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Birch_Aquarium_Entrance.JPG -->

entries.name: Mystic Aquarium
entries.desc: One of a handful of US aquariums housing beluga whales, set in the historic maritime village of Mystic, with a strong research and rescue mission alongside its exhibits on ocean life and sea lions.

photo.alt: Beluga whale greeting a child at Mystic Aquarium
photo.credit: <!-- photo: mystic-aquarium.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Mystic Aquarium, Mystic, Connecticut LCCN2012630593.tif" by Carol M. Highsmith · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mystic_Aquarium,_Mystic,_Connecticut_LCCN2012630593.tif -->

entries.name: Seattle Aquarium
entries.desc: On Seattle's waterfront pier with views of Elliott Bay, it focuses on the marine life of Puget Sound and the Pacific Northwest, with a large open-ocean tank and a new Ocean Pavilion opened in 2024.

photo.alt: Seattle Aquarium exterior on Pier 59
photo.credit: <!-- photo: seattle-aquarium-pier-59.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Seattle Aquarium exterior, May 2018.jpg" by Cody Logan · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Seattle_Aquarium_exterior,_May_2018.jpg -->

entries.name: Aquarium of the Pacific
entries.desc: Located on the Long Beach waterfront, it focuses on the marine life of the Pacific Ocean, arranged into regional galleries and a large open-ocean exhibit.

photo.alt: Aquarium of the Pacific's Pacific Visions wing, Long Beach
photo.credit: <!-- photo: aquarium-of-the-pacific-long-beach.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Aquarium of the Pacific 2024 February.jpg" by Jengod · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Aquarium_of_the_Pacific_2024_February.jpg -->
